quoteFirst: “On Tuesday, the 10th of July, 1584, at about
quoteLast: , the Prince, with his wife on his arm, and followed by the ladies and gentlemen of his family, was going to the dining-room. William the Silent was dressed upon that day, according to his usual custom, in very plain fashion. He wore a wide-leaved, loosely-shaped hat of dark felt, with a silken cord round the crown--such as had been worn by the Beggars in the early days of the revolt.
